purpose: Quality and historical-suitability gates for replacing visible procedural scenery in the January 6, 2021 Capitol scene.

global_acceptance_gates.close_view_mesh: Authored silhouette and surface geometry; no primitive/blockout replacement within first-person inspection range.
global_acceptance_gates.materials: Authored PBR response with base color, normal, and roughness data; metallic data where physically applicable.
global_acceptance_gates.textures: Use the source pack's highest practical texture tier for hero assets; do not upscale generated flat-color maps and call them high resolution.
global_acceptance_gates.rendering: Nanite or a verified LOD chain for dense static meshes; foliage must retain working wind and masked-material behavior.
global_acceptance_gates.history: Placement and visible configuration must be plausible for Washington, DC at 2021-01-06 11:50 America/New_York.
global_acceptance_gates.verification: Capture a first-person viewport image before accepting a live replacement; reject overlap, floating pivots, wrong scale, obvious repetition, or seasonal mismatch.
global_acceptance_gates.licensing: Retain Fab entitlement or explicit source license/attribution evidence before versioning third-party content.

installed_candidates.use: Closely mown, dense winter turf on grassable Capitol grounds only.
constraints: Tint toward muted cool green and dormant straw rather than saturated summer green.
Keep hardscape, roads, paths, planting beds, pool, and building footprints excluded.
Use varied scale, rotation, and all three live-registry-verified clump meshes to suppress tiling.

installed_candidates.use: Leafless/retained-leaf winter field trees at the 16 established live placements and 44 generated public grounds allee positions.
constraints: Preserve the authored January material instances and four-level project-owned LOD chain.
Use deterministic scale and yaw variation on the schematic grounds allees.
Do not replace source-mapped context trees wholesale with a single species or claim exact species provenance for the generated allees.

installed_candidates.use: Selected high-detail planting-bed specimens where species and January appearance are defensible.

live_evaluation.result: All six variants loaded and 572 actors spawned with grounded pivots, plausible maintained height, and visibly varied silhouettes.

constraints: Do not scatter flowering, tropical, or fully leafed specimens merely because they are high polygon count.
Evaluate winter material appearance, physical dimensions, Nanite/LOD behavior, and draw cost in Unreal before placement.
Cycle deterministically through all six authored variants and scale each from its own mesh bounds to avoid obvious repetition.
Keep planting-bed species separate from the turf asset set and do not infer an exact 2021 planting plan from pack availability.

installed_candidates.use: Temporary authored PBR lamp candidate after baked placeholder faces can be removed cleanly.
constraints: Do not overlay it on the existing baked rectangular-globe geometry.
Treat it as a quality improvement candidate, not as evidence of an exact Architect of the Capitol fixture type.

installed_candidates.use: Conditional scanned surface candidate for reviewed historic brick facades such as the Capitol Power Plant; not an architectural module or an automatic acceptance.

historical_comparison_references.title: Architect of the Capitol - Capitol Power Plant
historical_comparison_references.rights: U.S. federal government work; public domain in the United States
historical_comparison_references.use: Pre-2021 facade color, bond, mortar, window-bay, cornice, and weathering comparison.

constraints: Do not apply until brick color, dimensions, bond, mortar profile, and weathering are compared against dated public facade references.
Use displacement or Nanite tessellation only after connected viewport and performance verification.
Still requires authored corner, opening, arch, sill, lintel, cornice, and roof-edge modules.
Replace the placeholder asset path with the actual Fab import path after installation.

required_replacements.required_result: Historically configured modular high-resolution Capitol exterior with authored dome, porticos, columns, stairs, windows, roofline, and stone ornament.
required_replacements.candidate: Leartes Studios US Capitol Exterior Environment

required_replacements.required_result: Modular PBR traffic signals, street lamps, benches, bollards, hydrants, signs, and crowd-control barriers with source-derived transforms.
Authored modular public Rotunda and chamber surfaces, furnishings, architectural trim, and lighting based only on public references.
Reusable high-resolution facade kits applied to street-level priority buildings while preserving historical footprints and audited heights.

Licensed high-resolution modular brick facade kit with authored brick and mortar geometry or displacement-ready material, real PBR maps, corner/arch/lintel modules, and historically reviewed bond/color variants for close-view civic buildings including the Capitol Power Plant.
constraints: Do not simulate final brickwork by tinting the generic masonry shader or upscaling generated flat textures.
Keep the Capitol Power Plant under generic context masonry until a reviewed brick kit is available rather than misclassifying it as Capitol limestone.
Validate brick scale, bond, mortar depth, openings, and January weathering in a first-person viewport before acceptance.

explicit_rejections.reason: Generic low-detail plastic construction barrier; wrong crowd-control typology and insufficient close-view fidelity.
Retain only as GIS-aligned blockout, collision, or distant context; they are not final first-person art.
